perturbation.original_text: Each of PictureTel and Polycom shall (i) use the Confidential Information obtained from the other solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, executive officers and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers, consultants and attorneys) acting on its behalf (collectively, its "Representatives") who in each case, in such party's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who are set forth on Annex A hereto, as it may be amended by the parties from time to time; (iii) inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially and in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (iv) take all additional reasonable precautions necessary to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information by its Representatives to any third party; and (v) be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of PictureTel and Polycom shall (i) use the Confidential Data obtained from the other solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, executive officers and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers, consultants and attorneys) acting on its behalf (collectively, its "Representatives") who in each case, in such party's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who are set forth on Annex A hereto, as it may be amended by the parties from time to time; (iii) inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially and in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (iv) take all additional reasonable precautions necessary to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information by its Representatives to any third party; and (v) be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" is changed to "Confidential Data" in section 1(a)(i), but the rest of section 1(a) still refers to "Confidential Information." This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the obligations in (iii), (iv), and (v) apply to "Confidential Data" as well, or only to "Confidential Information."
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of PictureTel and Polycom shall (i) use the Confidential Data obtained from the other solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and for no competitive or other purpose; (ii) not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party, except for disclosures to its directors, executive officers and representatives of its advisors (such as independent accountants, investment bankers, consultants and attorneys) acting on its behalf (collectively, its "Representatives") who in each case, in such party's reasonable judgment, need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction and who are set forth on Annex A hereto, as it may be amended by the parties from time to time; (iii) inform its Representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and direct its Representatives to treat the Confidential Information confidentially and in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; (iv) take all additional reasonable precautions necessary to prevent the disclosure of the Confidential Information by its Representatives to any third party; and (v) be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in section (i). However, subsequent sections (ii) through (v) within the same paragraph continue to refer to 'Confidential Information.' This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the obligations to protect confidentiality, prevent disclosure, and be responsible for breaches apply to 'Confidential Data' as well, since only (i) uses that term while the rest uses the original term, 'Confidential Information.' This misaligned terminology, as described in the provided definition, creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, hence leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except as required by law, neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any person (including, without limitation, any director, officer or employee that is not set forth on Annex A) the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements between them concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between PictureTel and Polycom with respect thereto, or that such party has provided to or received from the other Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party. The term "person" as used in this Agreement will be interpreted broadly to include the media and any corporation, company, group, partnership or other entity or individual.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as required by law, neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any entity (including, without limitation, any director, officer or employee that is not set forth on Annex A) the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements between them concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between PictureTel and Polycom with respect thereto, or that such party has provided to or received from the other Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party. The term "person" as used in this Agreement will be interpreted broadly to include the media and any corporation, company, group, partnership or other entity or individual.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 2, "person" is replaced with "entity" in the first sentence. However, the subsequent sentence defines "person" broadly. This creates an internal contradiction as it is now unclear whether the restriction on disclosure applies to all "persons" as defined or only to "entities," leading to ambiguity and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as required by law, neither PictureTel, Polycom nor their respective Representatives shall disclose to any entity (including, without limitation, any director, officer or employee that is not set forth on Annex A) the existence, status or terms of any discussions, negotiations or agreements between them concerning a possible Transaction, including without limitation any offer, letter of intent, proposal, price, value or valuation, or any similar terms, agreements or understandings between PictureTel and Polycom with respect thereto, or that such party has provided to or received from the other Confidential Information, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party. The term "person" as used in this Agreement will be interpreted broadly to include the media and any corporation, company, group, partnership or other entity or individual.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'person,' which is later defined broadly. The changed text replaces 'person' with 'entity' but retains the original definition of 'person.' This creates a contradiction because the disclosure restriction is initially applied to 'entity,' but the definition provided is for 'person,' causing confusion about the scope of the restriction. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, meaning the contradiction is present in both. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall keep a reasonable record of the Confidential Information received by it and the location of such Confidential Information. All written Confidential Information delivered by one party hereto to the other party pursuant to the Agreement shall be and remain the property of the delivering party, and upon the written request of the delivering party, the receiving party shall (i) promptly return such Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof, (ii) destroy or have destroyed all memoranda, notes, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, or other documents derived from or containing Confidential Information, and all copies and other reproductions and extracts thereof, and (iii) provide a certificate to the delivering party certifying that the foregoing materials have, in fact, been destroyed or returned, signed by an authorized officer supervising such destruction or return. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the confidentiality and other obligations hereunder. Each party will keep a record of each person, other than its Representatives, that is afforded access to the other party and/or the Confidential Information of the other party, and before such access is afforded, each party agrees to inform such person of the requirements of this Agreement. Each party will furnish to the other party, upon request, a written copy of such record of such persons, other than its Representatives, that have been afforded access to the other party and/or the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall keep a reasonable record of the Confidential Details received by it and the location of such Confidential Information. All written Confidential Information delivered by one party hereto to the other party pursuant to the Agreement shall be and remain the property of the delivering party, and upon the written request of the delivering party, the receiving party shall (i) promptly return such Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof, (ii) destroy or have destroyed all memoranda, notes, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, or other documents derived from or containing Confidential Information, and all copies and other reproductions and extracts thereof, and (iii) provide a certificate to the delivering party certifying that the foregoing materials have, in fact, been destroyed or returned, signed by an authorized officer supervising such destruction or return. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the confidentiality and other obligations hereunder. Each party will keep a record of each person, other than its Representatives, that is afforded access to the other party and/or the Confidential Information of the other party, and before such access is afforded, each party agrees to inform such person of the requirements of this Agreement. Each party will furnish to the other party, upon request, a written copy of such record of such persons, other than its Representatives, that have been afforded access to the other party and/or the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 3, the term 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Confidential Details' in the first sentence. However, subsequent references within the same section continue to use 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction: is the recordkeeping obligation only for 'Confidential Details', while the destruction and confidentiality obligations apply to 'Confidential Information', or are they the same thing? This introduces ambiguity and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall keep a reasonable record of the Confidential Details received by it and the location of such Confidential Information. All written Confidential Information delivered by one party hereto to the other party pursuant to the Agreement shall be and remain the property of the delivering party, and upon the written request of the delivering party, the receiving party shall (i) promptly return such Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies or other reproductions or extracts thereof, (ii) destroy or have destroyed all memoranda, notes, reports, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations, or other documents derived from or containing Confidential Information, and all copies and other reproductions and extracts thereof, and (iii) provide a certificate to the delivering party certifying that the foregoing materials have, in fact, been destroyed or returned, signed by an authorized officer supervising such destruction or return. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the confidentiality and other obligations hereunder. Each party will keep a record of each person, other than its Representatives, that is afforded access to the other party and/or the Confidential Information of the other party, and before such access is afforded, each party agrees to inform such person of the requirements of this Agreement. Each party will furnish to the other party, upon request, a written copy of such record of such persons, other than its Representatives, that have been afforded access to the other party and/or the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Details' in the first sentence but keeps the rest of the section using 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the record-keeping obligation applies only to 'Confidential Details' while the other obligations apply to 'Confidential Information,' or if they are intended to be the same thing. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because it uses multiple terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" does not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, other than, as a result of a disclosure by the receiving party or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the delivering party or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by the receiving party to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to such delivering party or its Representatives; or (iii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the delivering party, provided that the person providing such information to the receiving party was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the delivering party or otherwise prohibited from transferring such information to the receiving party by any contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Secret Information" does not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, other than, as a result of a disclosure by the receiving party or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the delivering party or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by the receiving party to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to such delivering party or its Representatives; or (iii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the delivering party, provided that the person providing such information to the receiving party was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the delivering party or otherwise prohibited from transferring such information to the receiving party by any contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4, the definition is now for 'Secret Information', but all other parts of the contract refer to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction: if information becomes public, is it no longer considered 'Secret Information' and therefore not protected, even though the agreement primarily refers to 'Confidential Information'?
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Secret Information" does not include information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, other than, as a result of a disclosure by the receiving party or its Representatives in violation of this Agreement; (ii) is or becomes available to the receiving party from a source other than the delivering party or its Representatives, provided that such source is not known by the receiving party to be bound by an obligation of confidentiality to such delivering party or its Representatives; or (iii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the delivering party, provided that the person providing such information to the receiving party was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the delivering party or otherwise prohibited from transferring such information to the receiving party by any contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information'. The changed text redefines this to 'Secret Information'. However, the contradicted text refers to 'Secret Information', and the explanation highlights that the rest of the contract refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a misalignment: if the contract refers to 'Confidential Information', but the definition now applies to 'Secret Information', there's a direct contradiction regarding what information is actually protected under the terms of the agreement. This follows the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: PictureTel has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Confidential Information contains material non-public information. Each of the parties acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities and (ii) familiar with the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder, and each party agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including, without limitation, Rules 10b-5 and 14e-3.
perturbation.changed_text: PictureTel has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Sensitive Data contains material non-public information. Each of the parties acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities and (ii) familiar with the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder, and each party agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including, without limitation, Rules 10b-5 and 14e-3.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 8, 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Sensitive Data' in the first sentence. However, the subsequent sentence continues to refer to 'Confidential Information.' This creates a contradiction: is the prohibition on using information in violation of securities laws only for 'Confidential Information,' or does it also extend to 'Sensitive Data'? This introduces ambiguity about the scope of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PictureTel has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Sensitive Data contains material non-public information. Each of the parties acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities and (ii) familiar with the Securities and Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder, and each party agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including, without limitation, Rules 10b-5 and 14e-3.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers exclusively to 'Confidential Information' and the Changed Text replaces this with 'Sensitive Data' in the first sentence while still referencing 'Confidential Information' later in the same section. Because the subsequent sentences still refer to 'Confidential Information', this creates a contradiction about whether the prohibition extends only to 'Confidential Information' or also to 'Sensitive Data'. The Contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which demonstrates that the Change has introduced an internal contradiction in this section. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology leading to in-text contradictions.
